task_completion.description: Create the shell wrapper script for the custom integration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the creation or content of the file '/var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack-alerts' (e.g., via cat, nano, vim, or echo) inside the wazuh manager container.

task_completion.description: Create the Python script for the custom integration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the creation or content of the file '/var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack-alerts.py' inside the wazuh manager container.

task_completion.description: Implement JSON parsing logic in the Python script to extract specific alert fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The content of the Python script must be visible, showing it reads the file path from the first argument (e.g., sys.argv[1]), parses it as JSON, and extracts 'timestamp', 'rule.id', 'rule.level', 'rule.description', and 'agent.name'.

task_completion.description: Implement the POST request logic in the Python script using urllib.request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The content of the Python script must show it reading the webhook URL from the third argument (e.g., sys.argv[3]) and making a POST request using 'urllib.request' (e.g., urllib.request.Request / urllib.request.urlopen) to send the extracted data.

task_completion.description: Enable and configure the integration in ossec.conf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the edited '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' file containing an <integration> block with <name>custom-slack-alerts</name>, <hook_url>http://webhook-receiver:9999/alerts</hook_url>, <level>12</level>, and <alert_format>json</alert_format>.

integrity.description: Verify the agent executed commands inside the correct Docker container.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal prompt or command history should show 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' or the prompt should reflect the container's environment, rather than the host machine.

integrity.description: Verify the Python script uses dynamic arguments rather than hardcoded values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script must use system arguments (like sys.argv[1] and sys.argv[3]) for the alert file path and webhook URL, rather than hardcoding the webhook URL or a dummy JSON payload directly in the script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely edited the configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the use of standard text editors (nano, vim) or valid shell commands (sed, echo >>, cat) modifying the actual files, rather than just printing a pre-written success message to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Shell wrapper path: /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack-alerts. Python script path: /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack-alerts.py. Wazuh config path: /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf. Integration name: custom-slack-alerts. Hook URL: http://webhook-receiver:9999/alerts. Alert level: 12. Alert format: json.
